Opened an 08 Cristal and had some Tagliolini all uova with a Tartufi Bianchi Crema, and lots of Les Pres Sales butter.
The 08 Cristal is absolutely delicious, with a super fine mousse, Still tight, with tart citrus flavors, just a bit of brioche, great length, what’s not to like😄. That said I still think the 2012 is a more complete wine with more potential.
posts.display_username: Daniel. W i l l i g e r

posts.cooked: Yes, the '12 is showing better now, but it’s hard to say it has more potential. I think we need to give the '08 some time and it will prove the better wine.

posts.name: J0hn J K@ne

posts.name: Nick Rutk0wski
posts.cooked: This is one of my favorite champagnes ever. Will have to seek out some 2012.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked:
2008 >> 2012 for me.

2008 > 2009 =~ 2013 > 2012 for recent vintages.

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked:
2008 is a legend and, like others, I think it will surpass 12/13/etc in time, even though Cristal has been uniformly excellent in recent years. We opened a '14 the other night that was superb and quite open.

I bought my '08 for $192/btl and should have completely backed up the truck. But it’s so good I still am on the hunt for more, even at the current tariff. Not many wines I’d rather have, even for $350-400/btl. It kinda reminds me of '96 Salon, which originally was $150-175, and then I recall waffling about buying some at $350-400 at some point. I didn’t buy any but obviously should have.

posts.name: J0hn
posts.cooked:
MChang:

2008 > 2009 =~ 2013 > 2012 for recent vintages.

Is this for tasting right now, or your opinion of the long term potential? I ask because every time I’ve tasted 2009 it blows me away and is still the best Cristal I’ve tasted. And 2012 is my least favorite so far.
I was loading up on 2008 DP for $145 while keeping and eye on the $200 2008 Cristal, thinking, “I can always grab that next time, besides 6 DP or 3 Cristal?” Of course the Cristal got scooped

posts.name: Jim Stewart
posts.cooked: I have a very similar question for Michael as JBrochu. I value his experience with these wines and would appreciate his best guesstimates on when to drink these vintages. I’m especially interested in the 2008 and 2013. Thanks.

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked: I think in general the best 2008s aren’t showing to their potential at the moment, which includes both Comtes and Cristal. They’re, imho, currently less than the sum of their parts. I’d rather drink 2009 Cristal right now than the 2008, even if in 20 years that won’t be the case. And right now the 2009 Cristal is fabulous.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: That is my rating for current (and likely future) drinking.

posts.name: Matt Porter
posts.cooked: Opened a 2008 Veuve Rose last month and it was fantastic. The vintage is strong even at lower tier producers. I think the bottle we tried would have continued to improve over the next 5 years. Did not seem fully mature yet.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked:
Here’s my general impressions of the wines.

2008 - laser like precision, racy, not quite as opulent as 09 or 12, but more elegant and precise, superb finish.

2009 - opulent, a bit less lithe and precise when compared to 08, but decadent and beautiful.

2012- round, open feel, more plush than the others, lacks focus and complexity especially when compared to 08 and 13.

2013 - probably the most acidity of these wines, almost too much for my taste, but very fresh and enough fruit to pull it off; potential is just behind the 08.

haven’t had the 14 yet.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: 08 cristal is the best of the big house 759ml releases. I said it.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked:
CFu:

08 cristal is the best of the big house 759ml releases. I said it.

I generally agree, right now I’d say Cristal is slightly ahead of comtes, with Krug, DP and SWC a little behind.

Salon will likely be the best in the long run for whatever that’s worth. Of course as you said that’s not in 750.

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked: I prefer the ‘08, followed by ‘12 rose, ‘08 rose, ‘14, ‘13, ‘09, ‘12 and then ‘13 rose. I was not expecting to like ‘14, but I was pleasantly surprised and I recently bought more. I had the ‘09 from magnum last night and it was good, but nowhere near the level of the ‘08 magnum from a couple of weeks ago. We also had the ‘13 1522 last night and it held its own against the Cristal. I am having both the ‘08/‘09 from magnum next week (living it up while on vacation) and maybe the ‘09 will show better then. I do think all of these vintages of Cristal are very good and some just need more time, but the ‘08 and the ‘12 rose are better than the rest. For earlier vintages, I really like the ‘04 and the ‘06, ‘02 has been hit or miss and ‘05 is just ok. I don’t have any recent experience with ‘07.

posts.name: Daniel. W i l l i g e r
posts.cooked:
Very interesting regarding the views of the 2008. This bottle was somewhat disjointed yesterday. A little left for tonight and now it has settled down and come together, although still a bit clunky.

For those who say give it 20 years, i.e. until 2042, have you much experience with Cristal 34 years from vintage? And have you had a Cristal not too long after release and then the same vintage 20 to 25 years later?

The only Champagnes I have had young and many years later were Krug 88, 89, and 90. The 88 was spectacular young and also beautiful recently. Other bottles were mostly delicious but still good young and good old.

After 45 years of exploring wine I find that if I like it young I buy it and age it. But this is mostly Burgundys from the 90’s and early 2000’s.
posts.display_username: Daniel. W i l l i g e r

posts.name: Jay Miller
posts.cooked:
My most recent experience with 2008 Cristal definitely seemed shut down compared with 2 years ago. Still very good but not as supernaturally amazing as it was.

I’m waiting 5 years before trying another bottle.

posts.name: Brad Baker
posts.cooked:
Cristal has been on fire for a number of years. My preference (only considering the blanc) for overall potential is:
2012
2009
2008
2014
2013

The 2012, 2009, and 2008 are all very close and you can easily make a case that any is better than another especially considering palate preferences. The 2013 and 2014 are a hair behind the other three and you can also make a case that either is superior to the other. The quality of all of these wines is phenomenal.

Moving forward I believe the 2019 will best all of these, but it is still years away from release.

posts.name: C Chen
posts.cooked:
I think the 14 is drinking best today. Had all the vintages you mentioned in the last quarter. For long term, it’s 08 no question for me. I’m less bullish on 12 as a comparison vintage for 08. Doesn’t hit nearly the same for me.

Anyway, 14s worth a try if you have some.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
They’re only four & five years apart respective, but I think you see the upward trajectory of Roederer’s farming in the form of greater physiological maturity in the 2012 and 2013 vs the 2008. The 2012 and 2013 both display more plenitude, and while acidities are analytically similar, those acids are “riper” and better integrated. While I’m a huge fan of the 2008, I do think 2012 and 2013 are a shade better, even if the vintages themselves in terms of potential quality were a step behind 2008—and I have drunk them side by side several times. I tend to think JB Lécaillon agrees with me on this, too, though I have never put it to him as a yes or no proposition.

It’s interesting to see that Brad ranks the 2013 so low. To me, it’s absolutely brilliant and is clearly set to age very gracefully. I love it for its crystalline, chiseled style combined with full ripeness.

Also surprised to see people preferring the regular Cristal to Cristal Rosé. Sure, the addition of the red wine does impart some structuring phenolics, but that’s really beside the point, as Cristal Rosé is also, and much more importantly, a selection of just a few of the best plots that go into Cristal. So I think of it as more of a “super Cristal” than as a rosé (indeed, it’s so pale that after 20 years on cork it isn’t easy to tell that it was once a rosé).

posts.name: Ian Dorin
posts.cooked:
Massive 08 fan. I think it’s the best one since the 2002.

I loved the 2012 early, thinking it would be on par with 02 and 08, but I’ve seen it slip a bit, while the 2008 has only got better and better with time.

I think the 2014 is the best after the 2008. Stunning wine.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked:
I like 08 more than 02, which is sort of unusual for me because I’m a big fan of the 02 vintage.

I hope 12 improves because I have quite a lot of it.

I think Zachy’s and some other places had it for 150 or so back in the day.

posts.name: J0hn J K@ne
posts.cooked:
William_Kelley:

Oh, well that’s for sure. Every time people complain about Burgundy pricing, I want to say, but what about Champagne. And Cristal is far from where I would start complaining, by the way—I’d struggle to think of a “prestige cuvée” that merits is price tag more than Cristal, indeed.

Oh I agree entirely. Cristal is a suck it up and buy it wine for me each vintage but it’s hitting right in my champagne sweet spot, and it’s extraordinarily good. I think the upside for champagne is that there is a large number of very good producers selling really high quality stuff in that $50-$100 range. I don’t have enough experience with burgundy (and maybe never will) to find them with the same ease and at the same price point and qualitative level. And while Burgundy may have some competition with some Sonoma Coast and Oregon, I don’t think Champagne is facing the same level of competition from other regions. Yet.
